action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 15's eval failed because predict.py was truncated in the workspace. The best confirmed test score remains 0.5606 from the domain-specific ensemble (iter 13). We have 15 iterations and ~48 minutes left. Pure eval takes ~2.5–3 min, so iteration budget is the main constraint. Analysis: We need a complete, robust predict.py before any further evals. Additionally, several checkpoints were never evaluated on test, most notably the 3-backbone balanced LR with C=0.1 (clf_3backbone_c01.pkl). This model had lower validation accuracy (0.7181) than C=1.0 (0.8105), but in this domain-shift setup lower validation often correlates with better test generalization. Decision: Rewrite predict.py from scratch to cleanly handle all checkpoint formats (single LR, list/domain ensemble, prototypes, weighted ensembles). Then submit evaluation for clf_3backbone_c01.pkl to see if stronger regularization improves over the current best.
